title: Transportation in the Marshall Islands
content: Railway s: 0 km Highway s: <br "total:" NA km <br "paved:" 64.5 km <br "unpaved:" NA km <br "note:" paved roads on major islands (Majuro, Kwajalein), otherwise stone-, coral-, or laterite -surfaced roads and tracks (2002) Ports and harbor s: Majuro Merchant marine: <br "total:" 342 ships (1,000 GT or over) totaling 14,471,690 GT/ <br "ships by type:" bulk 86, cargo 18, chemical tanker 31, combination bulk 4, combination ore/oil 7, container 69, liquified gas 8, multi-functional large load carrier 1, passenger 6, petroleum tanker 106, roll on/roll off 1, short-sea passenger 1, vehicle carrier 1 (2002 est.) <br "note:" a <a href="flag%20of%20convenience" flag of convenience</a registry; includes the ships of <a href="People%27s%20Republic%20of%20China" People's Republic of China</a 1, <a href="Cyprus" Cyprus</a 1, <a href="Denmark" Denmark</a 9, <a href="Germany" Germany</a 70, <a href="Greece" Greece</a 54, <a href="Hong%20Kong" Hong Kong</a 2, <a href="Japan" Japan</a 4, <a href="Monaco" Monaco</a 8, <a href="Netherlands" Netherlands</a 8, <a href="United%20Kingdom" United Kingdom</a 3, <a href="United%20States" United States</a 87, and <a href="Uruguay" Uruguay</a 1 (2002 est.) <a href="Airport" Airport</a s: 35 (2009), see <a href="list%20of%20airports%20in%20the%20Marshall%20Islands" list of airports in the Marshall Islands</a Airports - with paved runways: <br "total:" 5 <br "1,524 to 2,437 m:" 4 (Eniwetok, <a href="IATA%20airport%20code" IATA airport code</a ENT; Kwajalein, KWA; and <a href="Marshall%20Islands%20International%20Airport" Marshall Islands International</a, MAJ; Rongelap). <br "914 to 1,523 m:" 1 (2009) Airports and airstrips - with unpaved runways: <br "total:" 30 <br "914 to 1,523 m:" 29 <br "under 914 m:" 1 (2009)
